title: ModelManual Tauri IPC bridge + on-demand md projection + HBR-MAN-002 no-context harness
goal: Wire three deliverables for A.6 closure: (1) Tauri IPC bridge `kernel.model_manual.get/list_commands/search` per spec §10.15.2 (AC-MODEL-MANUAL-IPC); (2) `just generate-model-manual-md` recipe producing on-demand MODEL_MANUAL.md per §10.15.3 (AC-MODEL-MANUAL-MD-ON-DEMAND); (3) HBR-MAN-002 no-context-model harness per §10.15 that proves a no-context capable model can operate Handshake from the manual content alone.
expected_diff_shape: New Tauri command handlers in app/src-tauri/src/manual.rs; justfile recipe; new test harness src/backend/handshake_core/tests/manual_no_context_harness.rs that simulates a no-context model reading the manual and answering a scripted set of operational questions.
unblock_conditions: MT-010 manual content exists; MT-011 paired-diff hook is active so adding the new Tauri commands also forces a manual entry in the same commit; MT-012 self-consistency scanner is active so the new IPC channels resolve correctly.
implementation_notes: Three sub-deliverables, all in one MT because they share testing infrastructure. (1) Tauri IPC bridge — in app/src-tauri/src/manual.rs add `#[tauri::command] pub async fn model_manual_get() -> ManualJson` (returns full Manual serialized to JSON), `#[tauri::command] pub async fn model_manual_list_commands() -> Vec<CommandReferenceLite>`, `#[tauri::command] pub async fn model_manual_search(query: String) -> Vec<SearchResult>` (substring + tag search). Register them in app/src-tauri/src/main.rs via tauri::Builder.invoke_handler. Channel ids per spec: `kernel.model_manual.get`, `kernel.model_manual.list_commands`, `kernel.model_manual.search`. ManualJson and SearchResult derive Serialize. (2) on-demand md projection — in src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_manual/projection.rs implement `fn render_md(manual: &Manual) -> String` producing a structured markdown rendering (YAML frontmatter with file_id, file_kind=ModelManual, updated_at; <topic> tags per GLOBAL-GOVARTIFACTS-020 for each feature_group / command_reference section). Add justfile recipe `generate-model-manual-md` that runs a small Rust binary `model_manual_md_gen` (declared in Cargo.toml as a [[bin]]) and writes the output to `app/MODEL_MANUAL.md`. Per AC-MODEL-MANUAL-MD-ON-DEMAND the projection is NEVER auto-emitted — recipe is run only on demand; deleting MODEL_MANUAL.md never affects manual content. (3) HBR-MAN-002 no-context harness — manual_no_context_harness.rs runs an in-process simulation: it loads the manual via the IPC bridge mock, then asks a scripted list of operational questions (e.g., 'How does an operator start a governed session?', 'What command lists loaded models?', 'What is the safety constraint preventing model spawn outside SandboxAdapter?', 'How do I open the Diagnostics panel?') and asserts the answer can be derived purely from the manual text (no chat history, no provider memory). The harness uses a small scoring rule: each question has an expected substring in the manual text; the harness greps the manual for the substring and asserts presence. Per HBR-MAN-002 evidence_kind=no_context_model_operation_test this is the wire test.
risk_if_missed: Without IPC bridge the frontend cannot read the manual; without md projection on-demand the operator has no read-only reference; without no-context harness HBR-MAN-002 has no evidence.
validator_focus: All three Tauri commands resolve via the manual self-consistency scanner; md projection is run on-demand only (no CI cron); no-context harness scripted questions are documented inline so operators can review the coverage.

minimum_controls: On-demand md projection recipe is NEVER auto-run in CI (would create md drift).
No-context harness questions are documented inline and can be extended without recompiling.
Adding the three Tauri commands forces a manual update in the SAME commit (HBR-MAN-001 gate is now active).
